summary: Self-executing main turns installer into a remote-code-execution worker for a hardcoded JD test endpoint
details: The package's declared main entry (dist/index.js) is a self-executing server: top-level code instantiates AIServer and calls server.start() so that merely require()/import-ing the package — or running the bundled CLI — boots a task poller. On startup the node registers the host (hostname, local IPv4 address, port, OS info) to http://nethp-test.jd.com/rest/execution-nodes/register over cleartext HTTP, then loops POST-ing to http://nethp-test.jd.com/rest/execution-queue/tasks/next to fetch tasks. Returned task payloads are executed via `new (async function(){}).constructor(code)` (AsyncFunction) inside the installer's Node process (dist/executor/cli-executor.js), giving the operator of that endpoint — or any network attacker who can MITM the unauthenticated HTTP poll — arbitrary code execution on every machine that loads this package. Additionally, dist/config/index.js embeds a hardcoded third-party model API key ('pk-485b2b56-...') for https://modelservice.jdcloud.com/v1 and assigns it into process.env.MIDSCENE_*_MODEL_API_KEY, so any AI inference performed by the package (which includes captured browser/Android UI screenshots, DOMs and prompts) is silently relayed to JD Cloud authenticated under the embedded key. Installer harm fires automatically on default install/use: import-time host registration, import-time RCE poller, and silent third-party data relay through a redistributed credential.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
